message:
perf(semantic): make rayon prep opt-in — benchmarks show 1.2×–2.3× regression on cheap embedders

The rayon-parallel canonicalize+hash prep step was default-on. Criterion
baselines captured under tests/artifacts/perf/2026-04-21-profile-run/
against a 2 000-message mixed markdown/code/unicode corpus showed that
rayon's per-task scheduling overhead exceeds the per-message
canonicalize+hash cost on every batch size tested against the hash
embedder, producing a 1.2×–2.3× wall-clock regression. For the production
ONNX MiniLM embedder, prep is ≤ 1 % of total embed time so parallel prep
cannot buy meaningful wall-clock either. Flip the default to serial and
keep the rayon path reachable via an explicit opt-in knob.

- src/indexer/semantic.rs: rename parallel_prep_disabled() to
  parallel_prep_enabled() and invert the env-var contract —
  CASS_SEMANTIC_PREP_PARALLEL=1|true|yes|on opts in; everything else
  (including unset) uses the serial path.
  Update the call site in SemanticIndexer::embed_messages to pass
  !parallel_prep_enabled() so the `serial` flag into prepare_window()
  keeps its original meaning.
- src/indexer/semantic.rs: add embed_messages_golden_digest_hash_embedder
  regression test — a frozen 64-message corpus (plain, markdown, code,
  whitespace, unicode variants) is pushed through the hash embedder and
  the output is SHA-256'd. Any accidental change to the canonicalizer,
  the hash embedder's deterministic projection, ordering semantics, or
  the prep pipeline flips the digest and fails the test.
- src/lib.rs: update the robot-mode env-vars help blurb from
  "CASS_SEMANTIC_PREP_PARALLEL=0 disable …" to the new opt-in wording
  ("CASS_SEMANTIC_PREP_PARALLEL=1 opt in … default off: serial is
  measurably faster on the common cheap-embedder path"), matching the
  new default so operators see the correct guidance.
